name: advisory_lock_ttl_spec
prompt: I have a distributed lock system where agents acquire named locks with a TTL. If the agent crashes, the lock should expire and become available. Write me a TLA+ spec that models this and tell me what invariants to check.
expected_decision_path: shared mutable state -> YES -> TTL -> YES -> Advisory Lock Pattern (Section 1)
expected_outputs: MutualExclusion invariant: no two agents hold the same lock
TypeOK invariant with bounded clock and TTL
Crash action that changes agent state without releasing lock
Tick action that advances clock
LockEventuallyFrees liveness property with WF_vars(Tick)
Bounded constants: Agents={a1,a2,a3}, MaxTTL=5
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Using Nat instead of 0..MaxTTL for the clock
Omitting the Crash action (makes the spec trivially safe)
Forgetting fairness on Tick when checking liveness

name: crash_reap_salvage_lifecycle
prompt: Port Daddy agents register, send heartbeats, and can crash. A reaper detects dead agents after a threshold of missed heartbeats and puts them in a salvage queue. Another agent can claim the dead agent's work. Write a TLA+ spec for this lifecycle. I need to be sure that (1) no alive agent gets reaped and (2) at most one agent claims any dead agent's salvage.
expected_decision_path: shared mutable state -> YES -> TTL -> NO -> crash leaves orphaned state -> YES -> Crash-Reap-Salvage Pattern (Section 2)
expected_outputs: NoFalseReaping safety invariant: dead status implies heartbeat gap >= DeadThreshold
SingleClaimer safety invariant: at most one salvager per dead agent
Heartbeat action that updates the agent's timestamp
Crash action transitioning alive -> stale
Reap action with guard: clock - heartbeat[a] >= DeadThreshold
Salvage action that removes from queue and marks salvaged
EventualSalvage liveness property with fairness
BondedCommons worked example referenced as prior art
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Modeling SQL schema details instead of state transitions
Putting all actions in one monolithic Next clause without named sub-actions

name: counterexample_interpretation
prompt: TLC found this counterexample for my lock spec: State 1 (Init): lock.owner = '', agentState = [a1 -> idle, a2 -> idle]. State 2 (Acquire a1): lock.owner = a1, agentState = [a1 -> holding, a2 -> idle]. State 3 (Crash a1): lock.owner = a1, agentState = [a1 -> crashed, a2 -> idle]. State 4 (back to state 3). The violated property is LockEventuallyFrees. What went wrong and how do I fix it?
expected_decision_path: TLC counterexample -> PROPERTY violation -> lasso trace -> system loops forever without progress -> missing fairness on Tick
expected_outputs: Diagnosis: lasso at state 3-4 means infinite loop without Tick advancing clock
The lock never expires because clock never advances
Fix: add WF_vars(Tick) to the Spec definition
Explanation of weak fairness: if Tick is continuously enabled it must eventually fire
Reference to Failure Mode 2 (liveness fails despite correct logic)
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Suggesting to remove the liveness property instead of fixing the spec
Suggesting strong fairness when weak fairness suffices
Ignoring the lasso structure of the counterexample

name: escrow_bond_state_machine
prompt: I'm building an escrow system where agents post a bond when they claim a resource. The bond can be released (agent completes work), forfeited (agent violates terms), or expired (timeout). I need to make sure a forfeited bond can never be released and that the total value across all states is conserved. What should my TLA+ spec look like?
expected_decision_path: shared mutable state -> YES -> TTL -> NO -> crash leaves orphaned state -> NO -> Escrow State Machine Pattern (Section 3)
expected_outputs: State transitions: open -> bonded -> released OR forfeited OR expired
NoDoubleSpend invariant: forfeited bonds cannot transition to released
Conservation invariant: sum of all bond values across states equals initial total
Terminal state check: every bond eventually reaches {released, forfeited, expired}
Bounded time parameter for expiration
Quality gate: >1M states explored with zero violations
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Allowing direct transition from forfeited to released
Using unbounded Nat for bond values
